zotero.abstractNote: Fine-tuning large vision-language models like CLIP often leads to catastrophic forgetting and a loss of interpretability. We propose a novel fine-tuning framework that leverages Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) to decompose the latent space of CLIP into interpretable features. By applying sparse constraints on the latent activations during fine-tuning, we demonstrate that our method preserves the original model's robustness while enabling fine-grained control over the model's behavior. We show that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on several downstream tasks while maintaining high interpretability, as evidenced by qualitative and quantitative analysis of the learned features.
